key_insight: Cavity vacuum fluctuations can induce macroscopic polarization and magnetization in materials that have none in free space, with symmetry dictating when and how these responses appear.
engineering_relevance: This work provides a symmetry-based design rule for using optical cavities to tune material polarization, magnetization, and related transport properties without applying external driving fields.
method: Start from an effective photon-free quantum-electrodynamics Hamiltonian.
Use crystallographic and magnetic point-group symmetry analysis to identify allowed cavity-induced responses and their tensor forms.
Validate the predictions with QEDFT calculations and case studies in α-quartz and antiferromagnetic Mn3Sn.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports symmetry classification plus computed examples showing controllable cavity-induced polarization in α-quartz and out-of-plane magnetization with anomalous Hall response in Mn3Sn.
watch_item: The effects depend strongly on symmetry and cavity orientation, so practical implementation requires precise cavity/material alignment and reliable QEDFT-level parameterization.
paper_title: Macroscopic Polarization and Magnetization from Cavity Vacuum Fluctuations
